design_philosophy.core_loop: Fight progression through arcade ladder, defeating increasingly difficult opponents with chosen character, unlocking character endings and bonus content (Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: One-on-one combat execution against adaptive AI with limited defensive options; environmental hazards (Fatalities, Stage Fatalities) add finishing move expectations
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master a fighting game character's moveset and defeat the Mortal Kombat roster; execute iconic finishing moves and witness character-specific story conclusions
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Brutally asymmetrical AI with exploitable flaws (projectile abuse during backflip, walk-mirroring, corner trapping); rewards pattern recognition and AI abuse over pure execution. Locked character roster on SNES (Sheeva/Chameleon removed due to cartridge constraints) versus arcade version creates platform-specific identity

difficulty.lives_system: Single-player ladder with continue system; each match victory progresses toward final boss confrontation
difficulty.continues_system: Standard arcade continue available; progression resets on game over
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-ladder checkpointing; victory on each opponent required to advance
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Extra Master difficulty tier available. Computer AI exploitable via specific strategies (jump kick during backflip trigger, walk-range mirroring, corner pinning), allowing skilled players to cheese encounters. 2-on-2 and Tournament modes provide alternative progression paths with different difficulty curves

progression.upgrade_system: No character stat upgrades. Combo system expanded: chain combos now startable via jump punch or vertical kick, creating broader comboing opportunities. Move rebalancing across roster (some nerfs, some buffs, altered combo damage scaling)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures system: post-arcade or post-tournament completion grants selectable prize pool with bonus fights, cutscenes, or other rewards. Character-specific endings unlock via ladder completion. Kombat Kodes enable stage/fight selection without progression
replayability_hooks: Character roster cycling (Kitana, Jade, Scorpion, Reptile returning from MK2; 23+ playable characters)
2-on-2 endurance variant with up to 3 human players
Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures bonus unlocks tied to completion
Kombat Kodes for alternate stage/fight selection

world_building_techniques: Character selection screen biography text (limited to 4 returning characters: Kitana, Jade, Scorpion, Reptile)
Stage design as lore indicators: Scorpion's Lair/Hell, Jade's Desert, Kahn's Kave establish faction territories
Character endings triggered by ladder completion (reduced from MK3; no full-body portraits or story images in SNES port)
Stage Fatalities as environmental storytelling (e.g., Scorpion's Lair lava uppercut reflects Hell setting)
Background cameos: Cyrax stuck in sand in Jade's Desert references his MK3 story

boss_design.philosophy: Shao Kahn as final obstacle in arcade ladder; extreme difficulty spike with AI leveraging full moveset and aggression. Pattern-based with limited exploitable windows. Represents narrative climax (Emperor Kahn's invasion) rather than character showpiece
notable_bosses: Shao Kahn (final boss): Arcade ladder terminus, unlocks Lost Treasures system upon defeat

faction_indicators: Scorpion's Lair/Hell stage: Scorpion territory, lava environment signals danger/netherworld affiliation
Jade's Desert: Associated with Jade; Cyrax cameo stuck in sand references servitude/entrapment
Kahn's Kave: Shao Kahn's domain, emphasizes invasion threat
Blue Portal/Lost: Liminal space tied to Kahn's invasion narrative
danger_signals: Stage Fatality mechanic (environmental execution) as mechanical representation of territorial threat
Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures system implies post-invasion looting/subjugation
Returning villainous ninja (Scorpion) paired with heroic counters (Kitana, Jade, Reptile)
environmental_storytelling: New stage additions (Scorpion's Lair, Jade's Desert, Waterfront, Cavern) expand Earthrealm/Outworld geography
Stage cycling in arcade mode (UMK3 exclusive stages appear twice before MK3 stages) implies priority ordering of conflict zones
Background details (Cyrax imprisonment, lava rivers) reinforce ongoing invasion consequences

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Ultimate Mortal Kombat 3 SNES article)
wikipedia (Ultimate Mortal Kombat 3 - Gameplay, Release, Reception sections)
